Title: Insights into acylation mechanisms: co-expression of serine carboxypeptidase-like acyltransferases and their non-catalytic companion paralogs

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Nicotiana tabacum; Camellia sinensis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: N. tabacum cv. G28; tea cv. Shuchazao
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SCPL acyltransferase, clade IA group d; signal peptide; catalytic triad S-D-H; disulfide-bonded large/small subunits
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: flavan-3-ol gallate / hydrolyzable tannin-related galloylation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a galloyl group from 1-O-β-glucogallin to EGC or EC to form EGCG or ECG; can also transfer caffeoyl group from caffeoyl glucoside to EC to form EC 3-O-caffeate, but only when co-expressed with CsSCPL5.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation/formation of galloylated flavan-3-ols, especially EGCG and ECG, but requires co-expression with CsSCPL5 for detectable activity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in buds, high in young leaves, low in old stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In tea, highest in buds and young leaves; in heterologous Nicotiana leaves activity detected only upon co-expression with CsSCPL5
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: secretory pathway/endomembrane system inferred from essential N-terminal signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: native protein purification with MS identification; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; stable transgenic N. tabacum hybrid co-expression; in vitro enzyme assay; in vivo substrate feeding; site-directed mutagenesis; immunoblotting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsSCPL4 was identified from native EC-GT protein fractions as the catalytic SCPL acyltransferase involved in flavan-3-ol galloylation. 2. CsSCPL4 alone was inactive, but co-expression with CsSCPL5 enabled conversion of EGC+βG to EGCG and EC+βG to ECG. 3. Mutation of CsSCPL4 catalytic residues abolished activity, showing CsSCPL4 is the catalytic component. 4. Co-expression with CsSCPL5 increased CsSCPL4 stability and post-translational processing into mature active protein.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CsSCPL4 large subunit was detectable only when co-expressed with CsSCPL5 in N. benthamiana; enzyme also shows regiospecificity for cis-flavan-3-ols (EGC, EC) and not catechin/gallocatechin; βG can also be converted to DGG as by-product in co-expression system.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering tea polyphenol composition, but deployment likely requires co-expression/stacking with companion paralog CsSCPL5 to obtain active enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for galloylation, but formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter in Nicotiana transient/stable expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: protein MS identification of native EC-GT fraction; phylogenetic/genome mining; transcript analysis by qRT-PCR; no public accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SCPL acyltransferase homolog, clade IA group d/e pair context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates with DkSCPL1 in transfer of galloyl group from βG to EGC to form EGCG in heterologous assay.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required with DkSCPL1 co-expression for EGCG formation; alone inactive.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: secretory pathway inferred from signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; transient expression in N. benthamiana; enzyme assay with EGC and βG
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DkSCPL2 alone showed no galloylation activity. 2. Co-expression of DkSCPL2 with DkSCPL1 enabled EGCG formation from EGC and βG. 3. This supports a conserved co-expression requirement in persimmon homologs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper does not dissect which persimmon paralog is catalytic versus companion.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential paired engineering module for galloylated flavan-3-ol production.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Contributes to EGCG formation with VvGAT2 when expressed as signal-peptide-swapped chimeras in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Native constructs were inactive in N. benthamiana, but chimeric Cs4SP-VvGAT1 with Cs5SP-VvGAT2 co-expression produced EGCG, supporting a co-expression-dependent galloylation function.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: secretory pathway inferred from signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; signal peptide swap; enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvGAT1 alone or with native VvGAT2 did not show activity in N. benthamiana. 2. Signal peptide replacement with tea SPs enabled co-expressed chimeric VvGAT1/VvGAT2 to produce EGCG. 3. The data support conserved co-expression-dependent acyltransferase function in grape homologs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Activity depended on heterologous signal peptide replacement, implying processing/localization constraints in N. benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SCPL acyltransferase homolog with conserved S-D-H triad
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: flavan-3-ol gallate / hydrolyzable tannin-related galloylation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: galloylation of flavan-3-ols and DGG formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: With CoSCPL5, catalyzes formation of EGCG from EGC and βG and also DGG from βG.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Requires co-expression with CoSCPL5 for acyltransferase activity leading to EGCG and DGG formation.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: secretory pathway inferred from signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transcriptome mining; gene cloning; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; enzyme assay; UPLC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CoSCPL4 alone was inactive. 2. Co-expression with CoSCPL5 produced both EGCG and DGG. 3. This suggests a conserved paired SCPL acylation mechanism and possible relevance to hydrolyzable tannin biosynthesis in oil tea.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Oil tea tissues are rich in hydrolyzable tannins rather than galloylated catechins, and both CoSCPL4 and CoSCPL5 retain S-D-H catalytic triads.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptome mining of C. oleifera; no accession reported

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Nicotiana tabacum; Camellia sinensis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: N. tabacum cv. G28; tea cv. Shuchazao
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SCPL family clade IA group e; signal peptide; altered triad T-D-Y instead of S-D-H
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein stabilization and promotion of post-translational processing/maturation via physical interaction with catalytic paralog
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: galloylation of flavan-3-ols by enabling active CsSCPL4 enzyme formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes stability and processing of CsSCPL4 into mature active form
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential positive regulator/companion for EGCG and ECG formation despite lacking catalytic activity itself.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential companion required for catalytic galloylation; without CsSCPL5, CsSCPL4 remains unstable/unprocessed and target products are not formed.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in buds, high in young leaves, low in old stems
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In tea co-expressed with CsSCPL4 in buds and young leaves; in Nicotiana confers activity only when co-expressed with CsSCPL4
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: secretory pathway/endoplasmic secretory route inferred from signal peptide
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms pre-processing complex/interacts with CsSCPL4 pro-peptide as shown by pull-down and Co-IP
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression assays in N. benthamiana and N. tabacum; catalytic-triad mutagenesis; pull-down; Co-IP; immunoblotting; in vivo substrate feeding
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsSCPL5 was identified with CsSCPL4 in the native EC-GT fraction and is co-expressed with it in tea tissues matching EGCG/ECG accumulation. 2. CsSCPL5 alone has no detectable catalytic activity, and mutations in its altered triad do not abolish co-expression-dependent galloylation. 3. CsSCPL5 physically interacts with CsSCPL4 pro-peptide, increases protein stability, and promotes post-translational processing into the mature active enzyme complex. 4. Therefore, CsSCPL5 is a non-catalytic companion paralog decisive for flavan-3-ol galloylation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study terms CsSCPL5 an NCCP; whether it is strictly a pseudoenzyme or moonlighting protein is discussed. Mature-protein interaction was not resolved.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Must be stacked/co-expressed with CsSCPL4 for engineering galloylated catechin production; offers a new strategy using companion proteins, not just catalytic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter in Nicotiana systems
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: protein MS identification of native complex; qRT-PCR; phylogenetic/genome mining; no public accession reported

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: non-catalytic companion paralog candidate / paired SCPL component
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: co-expression-dependent support of acyltransferase activity with DkSCPL2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required together with DkSCPL2 for EGCG formation in heterologous assay; alone inactive.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent essential paired component for persimmon EGCG-forming activity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; transient expression in N. benthamiana; enzyme assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DkSCPL1 alone did not produce EGCG. 2. Co-expression with DkSCPL2 enabled EGCG formation from EGC and βG. 3. This indicates a conserved need for paralog co-expression in persimmon.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper does not assign catalytic versus NCCP identity between DkSCPL1 and DkSCPL2.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: non-catalytic companion paralog candidate / paired SCPL component
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SCPL homolog; altered catalytic residues discussed in paper
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: co-expression-dependent support of acyltransferase activity with VvGAT1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to EGCG formation only when co-expressed with VvGAT1 as signal-peptide-swapped chimeras; native constructs inactive in N. benthamiana.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential paired component under suitable signal peptide context.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene cloning; transient co-expression; signal peptide swapping; enzyme assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Native VvGAT2 alone or with VvGAT1 was inactive in N. benthamiana. 2. Chimeric co-expression with tea signal peptides enabled EGCG formation, supporting co-expression-dependent function. 3. The data imply that processing/localization and paralog pairing are both critical.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: VvGAT2 is discussed as having catalytic-triad mutation and as a possible pseudoenzyme ortholog similar to CsSCPL5.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: non-catalytic companion paralog (functional) despite intact catalytic triad
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SCPL homolog with conserved S-D-H triad
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: co-expression-dependent enabling of acyltransferase activity with CoSCPL4
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required together with CoSCPL4 for EGCG and DGG formation; alone inactive.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Demonstrates that NCCP function can occur even without loss of catalytic triad, broadening the mechanism beyond pseudoenzymes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transcriptome mining; transient expression in N. benthamiana; enzyme assay; UPLC-MS/MS
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CoSCPL5 alone was inactive. 2. Co-expression with CoSCPL4 produced EGCG and DGG. 3. Because CoSCPL5 retains a normal S-D-H catalytic triad yet still functions as companion, pseudoenzyme status is not necessary for NCCP function.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Important evolutionary point: NCCP function likely preceded catalytic-triad degeneration in some lineages.
